title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: fly_footprint() converts point centroids rectangular polygons representing estimated ground coverage. standard 9” x 9” (228 mm) negative used BC aerial survey cameras produces footprint width 9 * scale * 0.0254 metres. 9x9 format default (negative_size = 9) correct standard BC catalogue photography. full BC Air Photo Database records camera focal length per roll, field available simplified centroid data — override negative_size working non-standard format photography. Note footprints assume flat terrain beneath aircraft. slopes true ground coverage differs — downhill slopes produce larger actual footprint, uphill slopes smaller one. coverage overlap numbers downstream inherit approximation. Figure @ref(fig:fig-footprint) shows estimated footprints 20 photos. Notice centroids fall outside AOI footprints still overlap — fly_filter() method = "footprint" catches edge cases simple centroid--polygon filter miss. Estimated photo footprints (blue rectangles) centroids (red dots) overlaid Upper Bulkley River floodplain AOI. Comparison spatial filtering methods.
code: footprints <- fly_footprint(centroids) plot(st_geometry(aoi), col = "lightyellow", border = "grey40") plot(st_geometry(footprints), border = "steelblue", add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(centroids), pch = 20, cex = 0.5, col = "red", add = TRUE) fp_result <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "footprint") ct_result <- fly_filter(centroids, aoi, method = "centroid") knitr::kable(data.frame(   Method = c("footprint", "centroid"),   Photos = c(nrow(fp_result), nrow(ct_result)),   Description = c("Footprint overlaps AOI", "Centroid inside AOI") ), caption = "Comparison of spatial filtering methods.")

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: AOI multiple disconnected polygons (e.g. patchy floodplain fragments), minimal selection optimizes total area can leave entire components uncovered. Use component_ensure = TRUE guarantee least one photo per component running greedy selection: Compare Figure @ref(fig:fig-minimal) Figure @ref(fig:fig-components) — component-ensured selection covers disconnected floodplain fragments cost extra photos. Component-ensured selection — every disconnected AOI polygon gets least one photo greedy backfill.

# How many polygon components in our AOI? n_components <- length(sf::st_cast(st_union(aoi), "POLYGON")) cat("AOI has", n_components, "polygon components
") #> AOI has 34 polygon components  selected_ec <- fly_select(centroids, aoi, mode = "minimal",                           target_coverage = 0.80, component_ensure = TRUE) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Seeding 9 photos for component coverage... #>   9 seed photos -> 78% coverage #> Selecting photos (target: 80% coverage)... #>   10 photos -> 90.1% coverage #> Selected 10 of 20 photos for 90.1% coverage #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on cat("Without component_ensure:", nrow(selected), "photos
") #> Without component_ensure: 3 photos cat("With component_ensure:   ", nrow(selected_ec), "photos
") #> With component_ensure:    10 photos sel_fp_ec <- fly_footprint(selected_ec) plot(st_geometry(aoi), col = "lightyellow", border = "grey40") plot(st_geometry(sel_fp_ec), border = "steelblue",      col = adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.15), add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(selected_ec), pch = 20, col = "red", add = TRUE)

code: photos_12k <- centroids[centroids$scale == "1:12000", ] overlap_12k <- fly_overlap(photos_12k) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on overlap_12k #> # A tibble: 7 × 5 #>   photo_a photo_b overlap_km2 pct_of_a pct_of_b #>     <int>   <int>       <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1  699370  699365       2.05      27.2     27.2 #> 2  699370  699373       0.134      1.8      1.8 #> 3  699426  699425       3.92      52.1     52.1 #> 4  699396  699393       0.246      3.3      3.3 #> 5  699396  699421       1.5       19.9     19.9 #> 6  699419  699421       1.46      19.4     19.4 #> 7  699425  699393       0.676      9        9 if (nrow(overlap_12k) > 0) {   cat("1:12000 overlap range:",       round(min(overlap_12k$pct_of_a), 1), "% -",       round(max(overlap_12k$pct_of_a), 1), "%\n") } #> 1:12000 overlap range: 1.8 % - 52.1 %  photos_31k <- centroids[centroids$scale == "1:31680", ] overlap_31k <- fly_overlap(photos_31k) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on if (nrow(overlap_31k) > 0) {   cat("1:31680 overlap range:",       round(min(overlap_31k$pct_of_a), 1), "% -",       round(max(overlap_31k$pct_of_a), 1), "%\n") } #> 1:31680 overlap range: 1.9 % - 61.7 %

title: Airphoto Selection Pipeline
text: practice want finest-scale photos first, fill gaps coarser scales. Sort scales finest-first parsing numeric denominator: Figure @ref(fig:fig-multi-scale) shows result — finest-scale photos (blue) provide primary coverage, coarser-scale photos (orange) filling remaining gaps. Multi-scale priority selection — finest-scale photos first (blue), coarser scales backfill gaps (orange).
code:
sf_use_s2(FALSE) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off  # Sort scales finest-first scales <- sort(unique(as.numeric(gsub("1:", "", centroids$scale)))) cat("Scales (finest first):", paste0("1:", scales), "
") #> Scales (finest first): 1:12000 1:31680  target_coverage <- 0.80 aoi_albers <- st_transform(aoi, 3005) |> st_union() |> st_make_valid() aoi_area <- as.numeric(st_area(aoi_albers)) selected_all <- NULL remaining_aoi <- aoi_albers  for (sc_num in scales) {   sc <- paste0("1:", sc_num)   photos_sc <- centroids[centroids$scale == sc, ]   if (nrow(photos_sc) == 0) next    # Take all photos at this scale that touch the (remaining) AOI   remaining_sf <- st_sf(geometry = st_geometry(remaining_aoi)) |>     st_transform(4326) |> st_make_valid()   sel <- fly_select(photos_sc, remaining_sf, mode = "all")   if (nrow(sel) == 0) next    # Update remaining uncovered area   fp <- fly_footprint(sel) |> st_transform(3005)   fp_union <- st_union(fp) |> st_make_valid()   remaining_aoi <- tryCatch(     st_difference(remaining_aoi, fp_union) |> st_make_valid(),     error = function(e) remaining_aoi   )   remaining_area <- as.numeric(st_area(remaining_aoi))   covered_pct <- 1 - sum(remaining_area) / aoi_area    cat(sc, ":", nrow(sel), "photos (cumulative coverage:",       round(covered_pct * 100, 1), "%)
")    sel$priority_scale <- sc   selected_all <- rbind(selected_all, sel)    if (covered_pct >= target_coverage) break } #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> Selected 10 of 10 photos intersecting the AOI #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on #> 1:12000 : 10 photos (cumulative coverage: 60.7 %) #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched off #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_union assumes that they are #> planar #> although coordinates are longitude/latitude, st_intersects assumes that they #> are planar #> Selected 10 of 10 photos intersecting the AOI #> Spherical geometry (s2) switched on #> 1:31680 : 10 photos (cumulative coverage: 100 %)  cat("
Total:", nrow(selected_all), "photos
") #>  #> Total: 20 photos as.data.frame(table(selected_all$priority_scale)) #>      Var1 Freq #> 1 1:12000   10 #> 2 1:31680   10 sel_fp <- fly_footprint(selected_all) plot(st_geometry(aoi), col = "lightyellow", border = "grey40") scale_labels <- sort(unique(selected_all$priority_scale)) palette <- c("steelblue", "darkorange", "forestgreen", "firebrick") cols <- palette[match(selected_all$priority_scale, scale_labels)] for (j in seq_len(nrow(sel_fp))) {   plot(st_geometry(sel_fp[j, ]), border = cols[j],        col = adjustcolor(cols[j], 0.15), add = TRUE) } legend("topright", legend = scale_labels,        fill = adjustcolor(palette[seq_along(scale_labels)], 0.3),        border = palette[seq_along(scale_labels)], bty = "n")

what: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: Creates rectangular polygons representing estimated ground coverage airphoto, based film negative dimensions reported scale.

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
code: fly_footprint(centroids_sf, negative_size = 9)

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: centroids_sf sf point object scale column (e.g. "1:31680"). negative_size Negative dimension inches (default 9 standard 9" x 9").

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: sf polygon object CRS input, footprint rectangles.

title: Estimate photo footprint polygons from centroids and scale — fly_footprint
text: Ground coverage computed negative_size * scale_number * 0.0254 metres per side. Rectangles constructed BC Albers (EPSG:3005) accurate metric distances, transformed back input CRS. scale denominator parsed scale column string (e.g. "1:12000" becomes 12000). 9x9 assumption: default negative_size = 9 (inches) reflects standard 228 mm format used BC aerial survey cameras (e.g. Wild RC-10, Zeiss RMK). BC Air Photo Database records camera focal length per roll (Type 02 field 3.2.2) available simplified centroid data catalogue. working non-standard format photography, override negative_size accordingly. Flat-terrain assumption: footprints estimated assuming flat ground beneath aircraft. reality terrain slope changes actual ground coverage — downhill slopes increase true footprint (ground falls away camera), uphill slopes reduce . steep terrain typical BC valleys, true footprints may differ meaningfully estimates. Coverage overlap calculations downstream (e.g. fly_coverage(), fly_overlap()) inherit limitation.

what: Select photos covering an AOI — fly_select
title: Select photos covering an AOI — fly_select
text: Two modes: "minimal" picks fewest photos reach target coverage (greedy set-cover); "" returns every photo whose footprint intersects AOI.

title: Select photos covering an AOI — fly_select
code: fly_select(   photos_sf,   aoi_sf,   mode = "minimal",   target_coverage = 0.95,   component_ensure = FALSE )

title: Select photos covering an AOI — fly_select
text: photos_sf sf point object scale column (pre-filtered target year/scale). aoi_sf sf polygon cover. mode Either "minimal" (fewest photos reach target) "" (every photo touching AOI). target_coverage Stop fraction reached (default 0.95). used mode = "minimal". component_ensure TRUE (default FALSE), guarantee every polygon component aoi_sf covered least one photo running greedy selection. Useful multi-polygon AOIs (e.g. patchy floodplain fragments) small components might otherwise get zero coverage. used mode = "minimal".

title: Select photos covering an AOI — fly_select
text: sf object (subset photos_sf). mode = "minimal", includes selection_order cumulative_coverage_pct columns.

title: Georeference downloaded thumbnails to footprint polygons — fly_thumb_georef
text: thumbnail's four corners mapped corresponding footprint polygon corners computed fly_footprint() BC Albers. GDAL translates image GCPs warps target CRS using bilinear resampling. Accuracy: footprints assume flat terrain nadir camera angle. georeferenced thumbnails approximate — useful visual context, survey-grade positioning. See fly_footprint() details limitations.
